zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes FitzAlan於大約1290年出生在英國越可爾貝達爾時，她的父親Brian FitzAlan31歲，母親Maud de Balliol31歲。她於大約1314年與Sir Gilbert Stapleton結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1348年11月11日在她的家鄉享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes FitzAlan于大约1290年出生在英国越可爾貝達爾时，她的父亲Brian FitzAlan31岁，母亲Maud de Balliol31岁。她于大约1314年与Sir Gilbert Stapleton结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1348年11月11日在她的家乡享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes FitzAlan was born about 1290, in Bedale, Yorkshire, England, her father, Brian FitzAlan, was 31 and her mother, Maud de Balliol, was 31. She married Sir Gilbert Stapleton about 1314. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 11 November 1348, in her hometown, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes FitzAlan est née environ 1290, à Bedale, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Brian FitzAlan, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Maud de Balliol, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Gilbert Stapleton environ 1314. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 11 novembre 1348, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes FitzAlan um 1290 in Bedale, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Brian FitzAlan 31 und ihre Mutter Maud de Balliol 31. Sie hat um 1314 Sir Gilbert Stapleton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 11. November 1348 im Alter von 59 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes FitzAlan è nata il circa 1290 nel Bedale, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Brian FitzAlan, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Maud de Balliol, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato Sir Gilbert Stapleton circa 1314. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 11 novembre 1348, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes FitzAlanが約1290年にイギリスヨークシュアイアベダルで生まれた時，父Brian FitzAlanは31歳，母Maud de Balliolは31歳であった。約1314年にSir Gilbert Stapletonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1348年11月11日に故郷で59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes FitzAlan(이)가 대략 1290년 영국 요르크실르 베데일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Brian FitzAlan(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Balliol(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 대략 1314년 Sir Gilbert Stapleton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 11월 11일 그녀의 고향에서 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes FitzAlan nasceu aproximadamente 1290, em Bedale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Brian FitzAlan, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Balliol, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Gilbert Stapleton aproximadamente 1314. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 11 de novembro de 1348, em sua cidade natal, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes FitzAlan родилась около 1290 г. в местности бедеил, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Brian FitzAlan было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Maud de Balliol, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Gilbert Stapleton около 1314 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 11 ноября 1348 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes FitzAlan nació aproximadamente 1290, en Bedale, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Brian FitzAlan, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Maud de Balliol, tenía 31 años. Se casó con Sir Gilbert Stapleton aproximadamente 1314. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 11 de noviembre de 1348, en su pueblo, a la edad de 59 años.
